anti_patterns: Do not turn an existing requirement for examples or counterexamples into a duplicate checklist with no search transformation.
Do not import empirical estimation, identification, regression, or data-fitting workflow into a theoretical probe.
failure_modes: A failed numerical search is misreported as proof that no admissible open region or mechanism exists.
The selected cases all occupy one local cell and conceal reversals elsewhere in the maintained parameter domain.
non_applicability: The move is inapplicable when removed complexity is economically first-order or when simplification changes the solution concept.
The move must stop when numerical reliability cannot be separated from the economic structure being investigated.
operation_steps: Solve the smallest tractable cases and deliberately varied nearby instances while recording exact domains and solver assumptions.
Preserve strict crossings, perturbation margins, reversals, failures, and primitive-by-primitive ablations rather than selecting one sign.
Export only a candidate cutoff, ordering, statistic, equivalence, or functional form plus the analytic obligations needed to establish it.
moves.runtime_projection: Trigger when a general model is too opaque to reveal which primitives generate a result, or when an apparent effect may be a finite-case artifact. Construct the smallest tractable instances and solve deliberately varied nearby cases. Record strict crossings, perturbation margins, the exact tested domain, solver assumptions, and every reversal or failure. Remove primitives one at a time and search across cases for a cutoff, ordering, sufficient statistic, equivalence, or functional form. Export only that candidate structure and the analytic obligations needed to establish it. Finite or numerical evidence is never a universal proof, and the move is inapplicable when simplification removes a load-bearing institutional feature.
success_diagnostics: A strict witness survives a declared perturbation and suggests one interpretable structure that normal proof work can test.
The output preserves tested scope and counterexamples and does not upgrade numerical evidence into a universal statement.
trigger_conditions: The apparent effect may be a finite-cell artifact rather than a strict result on a nonempty open parameter region.
The general model is too opaque to reveal which primitive, cutoff, ordering, or statistic generates the candidate result.

anti_patterns: Do not presume that posterior, cutoff, continuation-value, or dual representations are inherently superior to the maintained objects.
Do not use lower dimension, mathematical elegance, or shorter notation as a proxy for scientific contribution quality.
failure_modes: A notation-only compression is treated as a new mechanism, theorem, or novelty contribution.
The new representation suppresses a load-bearing institution or silently narrows the feasible set, equilibrium, or claim domain.
non_applicability: The move is inapplicable when the current representation is already minimal and exposes the mechanism without case enumeration.
The move must stop when the proposed map cannot preserve a researcher-owned primitive or other load-bearing institutional detail.
operation_steps: Re-express the same primitives through beliefs, likelihood ratios, cutoffs, continuation values, sufficient statistics, or dual objects.
Derive the map from the original formulation, state its losslessness conditions, and preserve the first compression failure.
Retain the representation only when it exposes an ordering, decomposition, comparative static, or transferable mechanism.
moves.runtime_projection: Trigger when the current model can be solved only by enumerating cases or when its mechanism remains hidden behind a high-dimensional state. Re-express the same primitives through a compact economic object: posterior beliefs, market-clearing cutoffs, continuation values, a sufficient statistic, or a dual variable. Derive the map from the original formulation, state the conditions under which it is lossless, and locate the first case where it fails. Keep the alternative only if it exposes an ordering, decomposition, comparative static, or transferable mechanism. If it merely shortens notation or suppresses a load-bearing institutional feature, return it as a diagnostic boundary rather than a promoted model.
success_diagnostics: The alternative representation preserves the maintained model and reveals an economic ordering, decomposition, or nearby transfer.
The first failed mapping is explicit enough to become a scoped boundary rather than disappearing behind new notation.
trigger_conditions: The current model can be solved only by enumerating cases and has no recovered primitive ordering or low-dimensional economic object.
The mechanism remains hidden behind a high-dimensional state, notation system, or application-specific formalization.

anti_patterns: Do not use analogy as a novelty verdict or as a replacement for closest-theory, proof, interpretation, or human investment review.
Do not add capacity, congestion, endogeneity, or another research object merely to rescue a weak or absorbed current mechanism.
failure_modes: Shared labels or mathematical form are mistaken for a primitive-level structural map.
The first formal mismatch is promoted despite leaving every prediction, mechanism, feasible object, and economic consequence unchanged.
moves.functional_name: Analogical Structure Transfer — First Mapping Failure
non_applicability: The move is inapplicable until one exact mechanism chain and one function-only comparator structure can be stated.
A mapping that requires a different research object must stop and surface the need for separate human reframe authority.
operation_steps: Map primitives, information, timing, feasible actions, strategic arrows, solution concept, and outcomes to one distant structure.
Mark each mapping exact, conditional, or failed and isolate the first economically load-bearing failure rather than shared vocabulary.
Construct the smallest variant isolating that failure and record which prediction changes relative to both structures.
moves.runtime_projection: Take one structurally distant model and write a one-to-one map from its primitives, information, timing, feasible actions, strategic-response arrows, solution concept, and outcome object to the current mechanism. Mark every mapping as exact, conditional, or failed. Ignore shared labels. Select the earliest economically load-bearing failure and construct the smallest variant that isolates it. Compare that variant with both the current benchmark and the source structure; record which prediction changes and why. Treat an exact mapping as absorption risk, not novelty proof, and a failed mapping as a conjecture, not a contribution. Return an analogy map, failure witness, minimal variant, and non-applicability note; do not choose routes or make investment decisions.
success_diagnostics: The map distinguishes exact common structure from one failure that changes a mechanism, prediction, feasible object, or consequence.
Exact mapping remains absorption-risk evidence and failed mapping remains a conjecture pending ordinary proof and novelty work.
trigger_conditions: Several proposals retain the same primitive-to-response chain under different labels or application nouns.
The candidate contribution may be a standard structure embedded in a new setting and its absorption boundary is unclear.

source_cards.citation: Hal R. Varian (1997), How to Build an Economic Model in Your Spare Time, The American Economist 41(2):3-10
does_not_support: The source does not claim that numerical examples prove universal results or that simplification should remove first-order institutions.
source_cards.functional_summary: The method essay explicitly recommends solving the simplest useful cases, comparing nearby examples, and simplifying before testing how far the discovered structure generalizes.
non_applicability: Do not simplify when the omitted institution or high-dimensional state is itself the economic object being explained.
source_cards.title: How to Build an Economic Model in Your Spare Time
source_cards.transferable_content: Use small cases to expose the operative pieces and errors, then export only the common candidate structure for general analysis.

source_cards.citation: Annie Liang (2026), Using Machine Learning to Generate, Clarify, and Improve Economic Models, accepted at Journal of Economic Literature
does_not_support: A computational pattern is not a theorem, economic mechanism, causal explanation, portability result, or evidence of reliable LLM novelty.
source_cards.functional_summary: The method article explicitly proposes predictive benchmarks and adversarial computational probes that locate model failures before human judgment turns patterns into interpretable economic structure.
non_applicability: Do not use a predictive or computational probe when no transparent outcome map exists or when solver error cannot be separated from economics.
source_cards.title: Using Machine Learning to Generate, Clarify, and Improve Economic Models
source_cards.transferable_content: Use transparent computational search to locate reversals, failures, or sensitive regions and retain the tested domain and assumptions.

source_cards.citation: José L. Moraga-González and Yajie Sun (2023), Product Quality and Consumer Search, American Economic Journal: Microeconomics 15(1):117-141
does_not_support: The final paper does not establish that its authors used computation to discover the result or that finitely many families prove universality.
source_cards.functional_summary: The published theory design preserves positive, negative, and ambiguous comparative-static cases while isolating conditions that organize their different signs.
non_applicability: Do not compare families that change the operative mechanism, suppress multiple equilibria, or remove load-bearing institutional detail.
source_cards.title: Product Quality and Consumer Search
source_cards.transferable_content: Vary an admissible family without changing the economic architecture, retain every reversal, and search for a general ordering or decomposition.

source_cards.citation: Emir Kamenica and Matthew Gentzkow (2011), Bayesian Persuasion, American Economic Review 101(6):2590-2615
does_not_support: The representation does not imply that all information problems are low dimensional or that it transfers without the paper's commitment structure.
source_cards.functional_summary: The paper represents signal choice through posterior distributions and concavification, with explicit conditions for when belief-space dimension can be compressed further.
non_applicability: Do not impose posterior-distribution language when the original environment lacks the beliefs, commitment, or plausibility structure it needs.
source_cards.title: Bayesian Persuasion
source_cards.transferable_content: Search for a natural economic representation, derive the exact map and feasibility conditions, and record where dimensional reduction fails.

source_cards.citation: Eduardo M. Azevedo and Jacob D. Leshno (2016), A Supply and Demand Framework for Two-Sided Matching Markets, Journal of Political Economy 124(5):1235-1268
does_not_support: Cutoffs are not universal sufficient statistics, and continuum uniqueness does not transfer automatically to finite or frictional markets.
source_cards.functional_summary: The paper represents stable matching through market-clearing cutoff vectors, making selectivity, equilibrium equations, and comparative statics more transparent.
non_applicability: Do not use the representation when stability, continuum, or market-clearing conditions are absent from the maintained model.
source_cards.title: A Supply and Demand Framework for Two-Sided Matching Markets
source_cards.transferable_content: Try a cutoff, price, or market-clearing representation only after deriving its exact relation to the original allocation problem.

source_cards.citation: Laura Doval and Vasiliki Skreta (2022), Mechanism Design With Limited Commitment, Econometrica 90(4):1463-1500
does_not_support: Canonical representation does not mean low dimensional, novel, or valid for arbitrary dynamic games and multi-agent commitment failures.
source_cards.functional_summary: The paper uses posterior beliefs and a canonical mechanism class to make hidden information feedback explicit in a limited-commitment dynamic design problem.
non_applicability: Do not transfer the representation without the information, type persistence, and period-by-period commitment conditions that support it.
source_cards.title: Mechanism Design With Limited Commitment
source_cards.transferable_content: Represent an implicit informational state explicitly only when the map preserves the relevant equilibrium outcomes and constraints.

source_cards.citation: Yuliy Sannikov (2008), A Continuous-Time Version of the Principal-Agent Problem, Review of Economic Studies 75(3):957-984
does_not_support: Continuation value is not sufficient in every dynamic model, and continuous time is not generally superior to discrete formulation.
source_cards.functional_summary: The paper uses continuation value as a recursive state that compresses history and exposes how incentive intensity and payment timing evolve.
non_applicability: Do not assume one-state compression beyond the maintained continuous-time agency environment; re-establish state sufficiency whenever its primitives change.
source_cards.title: A Continuous-Time Version of the Principal-Agent Problem
source_cards.transferable_content: Search for a recursive state or sufficient statistic, then state the conditions under which it preserves the original dynamic problem.

source_cards.citation: John William Hatfield, Scott Duke Kominers, Alexandru Nichifor, Michael Ostrovsky, and Alexander Westkamp (2019), Full Substitutability, Theoretical Economics 14(4):1535-1590
does_not_support: Formal equivalence is not novelty, and neither an arbitrary mismatch nor shared vocabulary is evidence of a useful new mechanism.
source_cards.functional_summary: The paper maps substitutability concepts across several market languages and shows where economically meaningful trading-network features require a broader object.
non_applicability: Do not transfer definitions beyond the domain, preference, and choice-correspondence conditions under which their equivalence is shown.
source_cards.title: Full Substitutability
source_cards.transferable_content: Build an exact structural map across distant settings and treat the first economically load-bearing mismatch as a candidate boundary.

source_cards.citation: Stephen Morris, Hyun Song Shin, and Muhamet Yildiz (2016), Common Belief Foundations of Global Games, Journal of Economic Theory 163:826-848
does_not_support: The formal foundation does not transfer to arbitrary games and does not turn a generalization or application into a contribution automatically.
source_cards.functional_summary: The paper maps special signal constructions to the deeper rank-belief properties driving selection and marks where binary symmetric scope stops transferring cleanly.
non_applicability: Do not import the mapping when action multiplicity, asymmetry, or population structure changes the belief property doing the work.
source_cards.title: Common Belief Foundations of Global Games
source_cards.transferable_content: Map the primitive or belief object that actually supports a result and record the first domain change that alters the prediction.

source_cards.citation: Kfir Eliaz and Ran Spiegler (2015), X-games, Games and Economic Behavior 89:93-100
does_not_support: Shared abstraction may indicate absorption rather than novelty, and the first formal difference is not automatically economically meaningful.
source_cards.functional_summary: The paper maps distant applications to a common threshold and externality structure while preserving examples that fail to enter the formalism or change its equilibrium implications.
non_applicability: Do not use the analogy when threshold, continuity, or preference conditions erase the current mechanism or manufacture a false isomorphism.
source_cards.title: X-games
source_cards.transferable_content: Use a distant function-only comparator and preserve the first failed primitive or strategic arrow that changes a prediction.

source_cards.citation: Ariel Rubinstein (2006), Dilemmas of an Economic Theorist, Econometrica 74(4):865-883
does_not_support: The source neither bans formal analogy nor licenses a mapping or model result as prediction, policy advice, or novelty evidence.
source_cards.functional_summary: The essay treats simplified models as limited fables and preserves dilemmas about absurd conclusions, evidence, regularities, and real-world relevance.
non_applicability: Do not convert the skeptical boundary into a universal veto against abstract theory, formal comparison, or function-only transfer.
source_cards.title: Dilemmas of an Economic Theorist
source_cards.transferable_content: Use mapping failure as a boundary diagnostic while remaining skeptical that elegant structure, formal unity, or analogy establishes relevance.
